header.title: Scientific Calculator — Advanced Math Calculator Online Free
header.description: Free online scientific calculator for advanced mathematical calculations. Perform trigonometric functions, logarithms, exponentials, factorials, and complex equations. Supports degrees and radians with a clean, full-featured interface.
header.keywords: scientific calculator, online scientific calculator, advanced calculator, trig calculator, logarithm calculator, exponential calculator, math solver, free scientific calculator

howToUse.title: How to Use the Scientific Calculator
howToUse.step1Desc: Click the buttons or use your keyboard to type mathematical expressions. The calculator supports basic arithmetic, trigonometry, logarithms, and more.
howToUse.step2Desc: Toggle between degrees (DEG) and radians (RAD) for trigonometric functions. Use SHIFT to access secondary functions like inverse trig and hyperbolic functions.
howToUse.step3Desc: Use buttons like sin, cos, tan, log, ln, sqrt, x², x³, xʸ, n!, and π for advanced calculations. The display shows your full expression in real time.
howToUse.step4Desc: Press = to compute the result, or use ANS to reference the previous answer in a new calculation. Press AC to clear everything or C to clear the current entry.

items.question: What operations does this scientific calculator support?
items.answer: It supports addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, trigonometric functions (sin, cos, tan), logarithms (log, ln), exponentials (xʸ, eˣ), square roots, factorials, percentages, and constants like π and e.

items.question: How do I switch between degrees and radians?
items.answer: Use the DEG/RAD toggle button on the calculator. DEG is for angle measurements in degrees, RAD is for radians. This affects trigonometric function calculations.

items.question: Is the scientific calculator free to use?
items.answer: Yes, this scientific calculator is completely free to use with no limitations, registration, or hidden fees. All functions are available online.

items.question: Can I use my keyboard instead of clicking buttons?
items.answer: Yes, the calculator supports keyboard input. You can type numbers, operators (+, -, *, /), decimal points, and function names directly using your keyboard.

items.question: How accurate are the calculations?
items.answer: The calculator uses JavaScript's floating-point arithmetic with high precision, suitable for most educational, professional, and everyday use cases.

items.question: Does it support parentheses and order of operations?
items.answer: Yes, the calculator correctly handles parentheses and follows standard order of operations (PEMDAS/BODMAS).
